Patch Set 1:

I would prefer to land the fix directly, since that avoids (further) complicating the patch backporting.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> do we want to revert it first and then apply fix along with new patch?
> I mean https://review.whamcloud.com/56664 is ready to go and should fix related crashes

According to Peter's weekly update email, it seems Oleg wants to do further evaluation/testing before landing the fix. Pushing a revert patch is another option to consider.
Hi Oleg, could you please advise?

Patch Set 1:

> Pushing a revert patch is another option to consider.

Isn't this patch *already* the revert patch?  It is Mike's patch https://review.whamcloud.com/56664 that I think will fix this issue.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > do we want to revert it first and then apply fix along with new patch?
> > I mean https://review.whamcloud.com/56664 is ready to go and should fix related crashes
> 
> According to Peter's weekly update email, it seems Oleg wants to do further evaluation/testing before landing the fix. Pushing a revert patch is another option to consider.
> Hi Oleg, could you please advise?

Andreas

This is just a "plan B" option that we are keeping available just in case. Mike has agreed that this could be a good idea.

Peter

commit.subject: LU-17906 pltrpc: Revert "LU-17906 pltrpc: don't use non-uptodate peer at connect"
commit.message:
LU-17906 pltrpc: Revert "LU-17906 pltrpc: don't use non-uptodate peer at connect"

This reverts commit 6fe522d3d4f92aa2a48a573419f4590b10ef13d3.
The commit caused many regression failures: LU-18365, LU-18367,
LU-18368, LU-18366, etc.

Change-Id: Iaf7a0ec7606dab7a1b4d5b9f4fd1a24eb2c8d94d
Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56736
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
